**

==Further reading==
* ''Cyclopedia of American agriculture; a popular survey of agricultural conditions,'' ed by [[Liberty Hyde Bailey]], 4 vol 1907-1909. [https://archive.org/search?query=title%3A%28Cyclopedia+agriculture%29++and+HYDE&sort=-date online], highly useful compendium
* Adams, Jane. ''The Transformation of Rural Life: Southern Illinois, 1890–1990'' (U of North Carolina Press, 1994) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Transformation_of_Rural_Life/ZrAoAwAAQBAJ?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=intitle:Illinois+inauthor:jane+inauthor:adams&printsec=frontcover online]
* Anderson, Rodney, ed. ''The Rural Midwest Since World War II'' (Northern Illinois UP, 2014). [https://www.jstor.org/stable/10.7591/j.ctvw1d4sf  online]
* Atherton, Lewis E. "The services of the frontier merchant." ''Mississippi Valley Historical Review'' 24.2 (1937): 153-170. [https://www.jstor.org/stable/1892076 online]
* Atherton, Lewis E. ''The Southern Country Store, 1800-1860'' (LSU Press, 1949) [https://archive.org/details/southern-country-store online] 
* Ayers, Edward L. ''The Promise of the New South: Life After Reconstruction'' (2007) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Promise_of_the_New_South/NF4vDwAAQBAJ?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=intitle:%22promise+of+the+new+south%22&printsec=frontcover online] 
* Barron, Hal S. ''Mixed Harvest: The Second Great Transformation in the Rural North, 1870-1930'' (1997) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/Mixed_Harvest/nyVQdkrNnskC?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=inauthor:hal+inauthor:Barron&printsec=frontcover online copy of the book] see also [https://networks.h-net.org/node/16806/reviews/18647/swierenga-barron-mixed-harvest-second-great-transformation-rural-north online review of this book]
* Benedict, Murray R, ''Farm Policies of the United States, 1790-1950: A Study of Their Origins and Development'' (1953) [https://books.google.com/books?id=AJUqAAAAMAAJ&q=intitle:1790+intitle:1950+intitle:farm&dq=intitle:1790+intitle:1950+intitle:farm&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjVm7OVufmJAxXdrokEHaK_IM8Q6AF6BAgGEAI online]
* Bidwell, Percy Wells, and John I. Falconer. ''History of Agriculture in the Northern United States 1620-1860'' (1941) [https://archive.org/details/historyofagricul0000perc online]
*  Bollinger, Holly. ''Outhouses'' (2005) [https://books.google.com/books?id=DQNR3uSBMS8C&printsec=frontcover&dq=outhouse+bollinger&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwjo1cPW4_GJAxWUk4kEHXiWC0kQ6AF6BAgEEAI#v=onepage&q&f=false online]
* Bowers, William L. ''The Country Life Movement in America, 1900-1920'' (1974).
** Bowers, William L. "Country-life reform, 1900-1920: A neglected aspect of Progressive Era history." ''Agricultural History'' 45.3 (1971): 211-221. [https://www.jstor.org/stable/3741982 online]
* Browne, William P., et al. ''Sacred cows and hot potatoes: agrarian myths and agricultural policy'' (CRC Press, 2019) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=hQSiDwAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PT12&dq=%22sacred+cows+and+hot+potatoes%22&ots=797WIwe-Tj&sig=zx0V6VeDCU_wzl7darRoglPbKE8 online].

* Brunner, Edmund deS. ''Village communities'' (1928) [https://archive.org/details/villagecommuniti00brun/page/n14/mode/1up online]
* Brunner, Edmund deS. ''American agricultural villages'' (1927) [https://archive.org/details/americanagricult00brun/page/n12/mode/1up online]
* Bull, Jacqueline P. “The General Merchant in the Economic History of the New South.” ''Journal of Southern History'' 18#1 (1952), pp. 37–59. [https://doi.org/10.2307/2954791 online] 

* Bushman, Richard L. ''The American Farmer in the Eighteenth Century: A Social and Cultural History'' (Yale University Press, 2018) [https://books.google.com/books?id=34taDwAAQBAJ&printsec=frontcover&dq=intitle:FARMER+inauthor:Bushman+inauthor:Richard+inauthor:L&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=1&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwi3leKY-ryJAxUOGtAFHYc-FOIQ6AF6BAgHEAI online]
* Christensen, Karen, and David Levinson, eds. ''The encyclopedia of community: From the village to the virtual world '' (4 vol. Sage, 2003 ) ISBN 0–7619–2598–8

* Conn, Steven. ''The Lies of the Land: Seeing Rural America for What It Is—and Isn’t'' (University of Chicago Press, 2023)
* Craig, Steve.  ''Out of the Dark: A History of Radio and Rural America'' (2009)
* [[William Cronon|Cronon, William]].  ''[[Changes in the Land: Indians, Colonists and the Ecology of New England]]'' (Hill and Wang, 1983)
* [[William Cronon|Cronon, William]]. ''Nature's Metropolis: Chicago and the Great West'' (W.W. Norton, 1991),  major scholarly study of Chicago's relationship to its vast rural hinterland
* Currid-Halkett, Elizabeth. ''The Overlooked Americans: The Resilience of our Rural Towns '' (2023), quantitative sociology
* Danbom, David B. ''Born in the Country: A History of Rural America'' (3rd ed. 1995) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=le05DwAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PR7&dq=%27%27Born+in+the+Country:+A+History+of+Rural+America%27%27&ots=BRHg3MWYZY&sig=NP_LTljSJ7XA-3t7wAy4JNcslRw online]; focus on economics of farming.
**Danbom, David B. "Rural education reform and the country life movement, 1900-1920." ''Agricultural History'' 53.2 (1979): 462-474. [https://www.jstor.org/stable/3741982 online]
* Dant, Sara. ''Losing Eden: An Environmental History of the American West.'' (University of Nebraska Press, 2023). [https://books.google.com/books?id=0Rq8EAAAQBAJ&pg=PA1 online], also see [http://www.h-net.org/reviews/showrev.php?id=59664 online book review]
* Davis, James C., et al. "Rural America at a Glance: 2023 Edition" ''Economic Information Bulletin No. 261'' November 2023) [http://www.ers.usda.gov/publications/pub-details?pubid=107837&v=9520 online from U.S. Department of Agriculture.]
* Dollard, John. ''Caste and Class in a Southern Town'' (1937) [https://ia801005.us.archive.org/9/items/casteandclassinasoutherntownbyjohndollard/Caste_and_Class_in_a_Southern_Town_by_John_Dollard.pdf online]
* Eberhardt, Mark Stephen. ''Health, United States, 2001: Urban and rural health chartbook'' (National Center for Health Statistics, 2001) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=n64ofQ0TfCUC&oi=fnd&pg=PR8&dq=rural+health+statistics&ots=fGBZA_6cEk&sig=E5K6P4FTXfm64iYEz1dAnU6bO0g online].

* Fink, Deborah. ''Open Country, Iowa: Rural Women, Tradition and Change'' (SUNY Press, 1986). [https://archive.org/details/opencountryiowar0000fink online]
* Fink, Deborah. ''Agrarian Women: Wives and Mothers in Rural Nebraska, 1880-1940'' (University of North Carolina Press, 1992). [https://archive.org/details/agrarianwomenwiv0000fink online]
* Fite, Gilbert C. ''American Farmers: The New Minority'' (Indiana UP, 1981), covers 20th century. [https://archive.org/details/americanfarmersn0000fite/page/n6/mode/1up ONLINE]
* Fite, Gilbert C. ''Cotton Fields No More: Southern Agriculture, 1865-1980'' (1984) [https://archive.org/details/cottonfieldsnomo0000fite_e2l1/mode/1up online]

* Freeman, John F. and Mark E. Uchanski. ''Adapting to the Land: A History of Agriculture in Colorado'' (University Press of Colorado, 2022) [https://www.google.com/search?tbo=p&tbm=bks&q=intitle:Adapting+intitle:to+intitle:the+intitle:Land+intitle:A+intitle:History+intitle:of+intitle:Agriculture+intitle:in+intitle:Colorado&num=30 online]

* Friedberger, Mark. ''Farm Families and Change in 20th-Century America'' (University of Kentucky Press, 2021)
* Fry, C. Luther. ''American Villagers'' (1926) [https://ia600406.us.archive.org/2/items/americanvillager00fryc/americanvillager00fryc.pdf online], heavily statistical.

* Fry, John J. " 'Good Farming–Clear Thinking-Right Living': Midwestern Farm Newspapers, Social Reform, and Rural Readers in the Early Twentieth Century," ''Agricultural History'' (2004) 78#1 pp.34–49 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/3745089 online]
* Fuller, Wayne E. ''RFD, the changing face of rural America'' (Indiana UP, 1964) [https://archive.org/details/rfdchangingfaceo00full/page/n10/mode/1up online]
* Gates, Paul W. ''The Farmer's Age: Agriculture. 1815-1860'' (1960), a major scholarly economic history

* Gates. Paul W. ''History of Public Land Law Development'' (1968) a major scholarly history [https://ia801406.us.archive.org/28/items/historyofpublicl00gate/historyofpublicl00gate.pdf online]

*Gimpel, James G., et al. "The urban–rural gulf in American political behavior." ''Political behavior'' 42 (2020): 1343-1368. [https://andrewreeves.org/papers/urbanrural.pdf online]

* Gjerde, Jon. ''The Minds of the West: Ethnocultural Evolution in the Rural Middle West, 1830-1917'' (1997) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Minds_of_the_West/qDxRsvJ0zeUC?hl=en online] compares German, Scandinavian and Yankee farmers
* Goreham, Gary A. ''Encyclopedia of Rural America'' (2 vol 1997); 438pp; 232 essays by experts on arts, business, community development, economics, education, environmental issues, family, labor, quality of life, recreation, and sports.
* Hagood, Margaret Jarman. ''Mothers of the South: Portraiture of the White Tenant Farm Woman'' (U of North Carolina Press, 1939).
* Hassinger, Edward W. ''The Rural Component of American Society'' (1978), sociology textbook.
* Hathaway, Dale E. et al. ''People of Rural America'' (Bureau of the Census, 1968) statistical detail from 1960 census. [https://www.google.com/books/edition/People_of_Rural_America/C_qI3Qhi4ZcC?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=%22people+of+rural+america%22&pg=PA1&printsec=frontcover online]
* Haystead, Ladd, and Fite, Gilbert C. ''The Agricultural Regions of the United States'' (1955) [https://ia802906.us.archive.org/6/items/in.ernet.dli.2015.224610/2015.224610.The-Agricultural_text.pdf online]
* Hinson, Glenn, and William Ferris, eds. ''Folklife'' (The New Encyclopedia of Southern Culture, vol 14.) (2009)
* Hirsh, Richard F. ''Powering American Farms: The Overlooked Origins of Rural Electrification'' (Johns Hopkins UP, 2022) [http://www.h-net.org/reviews/showrev.php?id=58057 online review of this book]
* Holt, Marilyn Irvin. ''Linoleum, better babies, & the modern farm woman, 1890-1930'' (1995) [https://books.google.com/books?id=aaW9lqXV-kEC&printsec=frontcover&dq=intitle:Linoleum+inauthor:Holt&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=1&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiPtd6EuLaJAxV8ATQIHXmOGCYQ6AF6BAgHEAI online]

* Hudson, John C., and Christopher R. Laingen. ''American Farms, American Food: A Geography of Agriculture and Food Production in the United States'' (Lexington Books, 2016)

* Hurt, Douglas, ed. ''The Rural South Since World War II'' (1998)
* Jensen, Joan, and Nancy Grey Osterud, eds. ''American Rural and Farm Women in Historical Perspective'' 
* Jensen, Richard J., and Mark Friedberger. ''Education and Social Structure: An Historical Study of Iowa, 1870-1930'' (The Newberry Library, 1976), [https://eric.ed.gov/?id=ED125982 online]
* Jones, Robert Leslie. ''History of agriculture in Ohio to 1880'' (1983) [https://archive.org/details/historyofagricul0000jone/page/n6/mode/1up online]
* Kirschner, Don S. ''City and Country: Rural Responses to Urbanization in the 1920s'' (Greenwood Press, 1970)
* Kirby, Jack Temple. ''Rural Worlds Lost: The American South 1920-1960'' (1987)
* Kulikoff; Allan. ''From British Peasants to Colonial American Farmers'' (2000) [https://archive.org/details/frombritishpeasa0000kuli online]
* Lauck, Jon. "'The Silent Artillery of Time': Understanding Social Change in the Rural Midwest," ''Great Plains Quarterly'' 19 (Fall 1999) [https://digitalcommons.unl.edu/cgi/viewcontent.cgi?article=1903&context=greatplainsquarterly online]
* Lingeman, Richard. ''Small Town America: A Narrative History, 1620-The Present'' (Putnam, 1980)
* Longworth, Richard C. ''Caught in the Middle: America's Heartland in the Age of Globalism'' (2008) [https://books.google.com/books?id=wqd5J53Oof4C&printsec=frontcover&dq=intitle:Caught+intitle:in+intitle:the+intitle:Middle+inauthor:Richard+inauthor:C+inauthor:Longworth&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=0&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiu9I_TiO-JAxUXOjQIHY0LAnMQ6AF6BAgFEAI online]
* Miller, John E. "Centrifugal and Centripetal Forces Shaping the American Midwest," ''Studies in Midwestern History'' (2015) 1#1 pp.1-10, on push and pull factors as farm boys left home. [https://scholarworks.gvsu.edu/midwesternhistory/vol1/iss1/1 online]
* Miner, Horace. ''Culture and Agriculture: An Anthropological Study of a Corn Belt County'' (1949) in -depth study of [[Hardin County, Iowa]] in 1939. [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=tiPgDwAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PR5&dq=horace+miner+agriculture&ots=cEOAtFMBbL&sig=fzDGej8djJhrdMesvH7GJvbivzo online]

* Mueller, K.J. "Rural health policy: Past as a prelude to the future". in Sana Loue, and B.E. Quill, (eds.). ''Handbook of Rural Health'' (Kluwer Academic-Penum, 2001)  pp. 45–72. ISBN 978-0-306-46479-9.

* Nelson, Lowry. ''Rural Sociology'' (1945) [https://archive.org/details/ruralsociologyru00nels/page/n8/mode/1up online]

* Percy, Thomas C. ''Kansas State Fair'' (Arcadia, 2014) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=7Ir_AwAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PA6&dq=%22Kansas+state+fair%22+history&ots=ILMQur86_Z&sig=Mi38OzFtuZ4-qPGQZcb1uNY17Ms online].

* Pritchard, James A. "A Landscape Transformed:  Ecosystems and Natural Resources in the Midwest." The Rural Midwest Since World War II, edited by J. L. Anderson, (Cornell UP, 2014), pp. 12–43. [http://www.jstor.org/stable/10.7591/j.ctvw1d4sf.7 online].

* Rasmussen, Wayne David. ''Taking the university to the people'' (Iowa State UP,  history of agricultural extension work [https://archive.org/details/takinguniversity00rasm online]

* Ricketts, Thomas C. "The changing nature of rural health care." ''Annual review of public health'' 21.1 (2000): 639-657. [https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Thomas-Ricketts-3/publication/12434083_The_Changing_Nature_of_Rural_Health_Care/links/02bfe514327e2d1c25000000/The-Changing-Nature-of-Rural-Health-Care.pdf online]

* Ricketts, Thomas C., ed. ''Rural health in the United States'' (Oxford UP, 1999) [https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=kEErphxU1m4C&oi=fnd&pg=PR11&dq=rural+health+statistics&ots=t3DAh9igpg&sig=gJD4PMI-sA4c_AydNc0bNLwfi_w online].

* Riney-Kehrberg, Pamela. ed. ''The Routledge History of Rural America'' (2016)
* Russell, Howard S. ''A long, deep furrow: three centuries of farming in New England'' (1976)
* Russo, David J. ''American towns: an interpretive history'' (2001) [https://archive.org/details/americantownsint0000russ/page/n6/mode/1up online]
* [[Theodore Saloutos|Saloutos, Theodore]], and [[John Donald Hicks|John D. Hicks]]. ''Twentieth Century Populism: Agricultural Discontent in the Middle West, 1900-1939'' (1951) [https://archive.org/details/twentiethcentury0000salo online ]
* [[Theodore Saloutos|Saloutos, Theodore]] ''Farmer movements in the South, 1865-1933'' (1964) [https://archive.org/details/farmermovementsi00salo online]
* Schafer, Joseph. ''The social history of American agriculture'' (1936) [https://archive.org/details/in.ernet.dli.2015.190571/page/n10/mode/1up online]
* Schapsmeier, Edward L., and Frederick H. ''Encyclopedia of American Agricultural History'' (Greenwood, 1975)
* Schob, David E. ''Hired hands and plowboys: farm labor in the Midwest, 1815-60''  (1975), pp. 173–249.
* Shannon, Fred A. ''The Farmer's Last Frontier: Agriculture, 1860–1897'' (1945) [https://archive.org/details/farmerslastfront00shan online]
* Sims, Newell Leroy. ''The Rural Community: Ancient and Modern'' (1920) [https://archive.org/details/ruralcommunitya01simsgoog online]
* Stray Dog Institute, "The Importance of US Farmers" (November 18, 2021) [https://www.straydoginstitute.org/us-farmers/ online]

* Taylor, Carl C. ''Rural sociology'' (1933) [https://archive.org/details/dli.ministry.22218/page/n7/mode/1up online]
* Tickamyer, Ann, et al. ''Rural Poverty in the United States'' (2017)
* U.S. Department of Agriculture. ''Farmers in a changing world'' (1940) 1240 pp of articles by experts in agriculture and rural life [https://archive.org/details/farmersinchangin00unit online]
* Vidich, Arthur J., and Joseph Bensman. ''Small town in mass society; class, power, and religion in a rural community'' (1960), in upstate New York [https://archive.org/details/smalltowninmasss00vidi/page/n14/mode/1up online]
* Vogt, Paul L. ''Introduction to rural sociology'' (1922) [https://archive.org/details/cu31924058841549/page/n4/mode/1up online]
* Walsh, Lorena S. "Urban Amenities and Rural Sufficiency: Living Standards and Consumer Behavior in the Colonial Chesapeake, 1643-1777," ''Journal-of Economic History'' 43#1, (1983), 109-17. [http://www.jstor.org/stable/2120269. online]
* Weeden, William Babcock. ''Economic and Social History of New England, 1620-1789'' (1891)  964 pages; [https://books.google.com/books?id=JUJaNzIMr44C&pg=PA1 online edition]

* Whelpton, P.K. "Occupational Groups in the United States, 1820-1920" ''Journal of the American Statistical Association'' 21#155 (1926), pp.335–343 [http://www.jstor.org/stable/2277062 online]
* Woodward, C. Vann.  ''The Origins of the New South, 1877-1913'' (1951) [https://books.google.com/books?id=NQ2pAgAAQBAJ&printsec=frontcover&dq=intitle:1877+intitle:1913&hl=en&newbks=1&newbks_redir=1&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiOzrr4oLqJAxXj38kDHc33CwMQ6AF6BAgLEAI online]
*  Wuthnow, Robert. ''The Left Behind: Decline and Rage in Rural America'' (Princeton UP, 2018) [https://www.google.com/books/edition/The_Left_Behind/d1iYDwAAQBAJ?hl=en&gbpv=1&dq=intitle:The+intitle:Left+intitle:Behind+intitle:Decline+intitle:and+intitle:Rage+intitle:in+intitle:Rural+intitle:America&printsec=frontcover online]
* Wyman, Andrea. ''Rural women teachers in the United States '' (1997) [https://archive.org/details/ruralwomenteache0000wyma/page/n6/mode/1up online]

===Historiography===

* Aley, Ginette . " 'Knotted Together like Roots in the Darkness': Rural Midwestern Women and Region: A Bibliographic Guide" ''Agricultural History'' 77#3 (2003), pp. 453-481 [http://www.jstor.org/stable/3745042 online]

* Ambrose, Linda M. et al. "Revisiting Rural Women's History" ''Agricultural History'' (2015), 89#3, pp. 380-387 [https://www.jstor.org/stable/10.3098/ah.2015.089.3.380 online] 
* Burton, Orville Vernon. "Reaping What We Sow: Community and Rural History," ''Agricultural History'' 76#4 (2002) pp.631–658. [https://www.ideals.illinois.edu/items/2445/bitstreams/8836/data.pdf online]

* Eagles, C. W. "Urban‐Rural Conflict in the 1920s: A Historiographical Assessment" ''The Historian'', (1986) 49(1), pp.26-48. [https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdf/10.1111/j.1540-6563.1986.tb02006.x online]

* Fink, Deborah; Valerie Grim and Dorothy Schwieder. "Introduction: Rural and Farm Women in Historical Perspective." ''Agricultural History'' 73#2 (1999), pp. 131-135 [http://www.jstor.org/stable/3744656 online], introduction to special issue.
* Garry, Patrick. "Cherished Lives and Lasting Values: Memoirs of the Rural Midwest." ''Middle West Review'' 10.1 (2023): 183-194. Reviews ten autobiographical memoirs from Midwest. [https://muse.jhu.edu/article/912196 excerpt]

* Hurt, R. Douglas. "The Historiography of American Agriculture" ''OAH Magazine of History'' 5#3 (1991) pp.13-17 

* Hahn, Stephen, and Jonathan Prude. "Introduction" in Hahn and Prude, eds. ''Essays in the social history of rural America'' (U of North Carolina Press, 1985) pp.3–24.

* Hurt, R. Douglas. "Writing Midwestern State Histories." ''Middle West Review'' 10#1 (2023): 195-201. [https://muse.jhu.edu/article/912197 excerpt]

* Merchant, Carolyn. ''The Columbia guide to American environmental history'' (Columbia UP, 2012).
* Swierenga, Robert P. “Theoretical Perspectives on the New Rural History: From Environmentalism to Modernization.” ''Agricultural History'' 56#3 (1982), pp. 495–502. [http://www.jstor.org/stable/3742549 online]

===Primary sources===
* Grant, H. Roger, ed. ''Railroads in the Heartland: Steam and Traction in the Golden Age of Postcards'' (1997) over 100 historic photographs. [https://archive.org/details/railroadsinheart0000gran online]
*  Kirkpatrick, Ellis Lore. ''The farmer's standard of living: a socio-economic study of 2886 white farm families of selected localities in 11 states'' (1926) [https://archive.org/details/farmersstandardo1466kirk/page/1/mode/1up online]
* Phelan, John, ed. ''Readings in rural sociology'' (1920), about the USA [https://archive.org/details/readingsinrurals0000phel/page/n6/mode/2up online]
* Phillips, Ulrich B. ed. ''Plantation and Frontier Documents, 1649–1863; Illustrative of Industrial History in the Colonial and Antebellum South: Collected from MSS. and Other Rare Sources.'' 2 Volumes. (1909). [https://archive.org/search?query=title%3A%28Plantation%20and%20Frontier%20Documents%29 online]
* Rasmussen, Wayne, ed. ''Agriculture in the United States: A Documentary History'' (4 vol 1975) 2800 pages of primary sources [https://archive.org/search?query=title%3A%28documentary%29%20AND%20creator%3A%28Rasmussen%2C%20Wayne%2C%29 online]
* Schmidt, Louis Bernard, ed. ''Readings in the economic history of American agriculture'' (1925) [https://archive.org/details/in.ernet.dli.2015.84830 online]

* Sorokin, Pitirim ''et al.'', eds. ''A Systematic Sourcebook in Rural Sociology'' (3 vol. 1930), 2000 pages of primary sources and commentary; worldwide coverage
* Williams, James Hickel. ''Our Rural Heritage'' (1925), [https://archive.org/details/ourruralheritage031720mbp/page/n3/mode/2up online] explores rural roots of psychology of typical Americans.

==External links==
* [https://networks.h-net.org/h-rural "H-Rural: H-Net's network for the study of rural and agricultural history], free discussion group
* [https://archive.org/details/CSPAN2_20220918_000000_Lectures_in_History_Rural_America_after_the_Civil_War  "Lectures in History: Rural America After the Civil War" (CSPAN. 2002) ]

*[https://archive.today/20130128192029/http://aghist.metapress.com/home/main.mpx ''Agricultural History'' a leading scholarly journal]
* [https://archive.today/20130223124941/http://www.aghistorysociety.org/journal/ Agricultural History Society]
* [https://digitalcollections.nypl.org/collections/the-pageant-of-america-collection#/?tab=navigation&roots=2:7ace7290-c611-012f-934c-58d385a7bc34 331 historic photographs of American farmlands, farmers, farm operations and rural areas; These are pre-1923 and out of copyright.]
* [http://www.umvphotoarchive.org/digital/collection/gales/id/392/rec/2 Upper Mississippi Valley Digital Image Archive], hundreds of photographs of rural life; copyright has expired.

* [https://www.tntech.edu/grand-challenge/pdf/Hill_History-and-Future-of-Rural-America.pdf "Literature Review: The History and Future of Rural America" by Amy Hill, Lecturer at Tennessee Tech, 2020).]

[[Category:Rural geography]]
[[Category:Rural society in the United States]]
